---
title: "Understanding Emotional Avoidance"
format: html
---


# Understanding Emotional Avoidance

Avoiding places that bring up strong emotions is common in recovery. It can protect you in the short term by reducing overwhelm and lowering risk.

However, over time, avoidance can:

- Make certain places feel more intense or scary  
- Limit your daily life  
- Prevent emotional processing  

The goal is to understand when avoidance is helpful—and when it might be holding you back.


## Use this worksheet to explore a place you avoid and understand the role avoidance is playing in your recovery.


### 1. The Place

- **Place I avoid:**  

- **Emotions it brings up:**  

- **Intensity (0–10):**   



### 2. What Feels Hard

- [ ] Memories connected to alcohol  
- [ ] Anxiety or stress  
- [ ] Sadness or grief  
- [ ] Shame or regret  
- [ ] Fear of urges  
- [ ] Other: _____________  



### 3. My Current Pattern

- [ ] I always avoid it  
- [ ] I sometimes avoid it  
- [ ] I go but feel uncomfortable  



### 4. Short-Term vs Long-Term

- Short-term benefit of avoiding:

- Long-term cost of avoiding:



## 5. Reflection

- Is this avoidance helping me right now?  

- Might this change in the future?  


![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/window.jpg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

**Remember: Avoidance can be protective—but it works best when it is a conscious choice, not an automatic habit.**
